sections.title: Overview
sections.content: The Zoom connector syncs users, meetings, cloud recordings, and webinars from Zoom into the Shielva knowledge base using the Zoom REST API v2. It uses Server-to-Server OAuth (account_credentials grant) — no user redirect needed. Credentials are exchanged for a Bearer token automatically, and refreshed every hour. The connector implements cursor-based pagination with next_page_token, exponential backoff retry (3 attempts), rate-limit back-off, and a circuit breaker. Each document receives a stable SHA-256-based ID for idempotent syncs.

subsections.title: Server-to-Server OAuth
subsections.content: The connector uses Zoom Server-to-Server OAuth (account_credentials grant). Create a Server-to-Server OAuth App in the Zoom Marketplace, add the required admin scopes, and provide the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret in Shielva. No user redirect or browser consent is required.
steps: Go to marketplace.zoom.us → Develop → Build App → Server-to-Server OAuth.
Note the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret from App Credentials.
Enter the Account ID, Client ID, and Client Secret in Shielva.
Shielva exchanges credentials for a token and probes GET /accounts/me.

fields.label: Account ID
fields.description: Your Zoom Account ID, found on the App Credentials page in Zoom Marketplace or under Account Profile in the Zoom Admin portal.

fields.label: Client ID
fields.description: OAuth Client ID from Zoom Marketplace → Your Server-to-Server OAuth App → App Credentials.

fields.label: Client Secret
fields.description: OAuth Client Secret from Zoom Marketplace → Your Server-to-Server OAuth App → App Credentials.

sections.title: Sync Engine
sections.content: The sync() method performs a full paginated sync of Zoom meetings and cloud recordings. For each resource type, it fetches all pages using the next_page_token cursor. Each raw record is passed through a normalizer that converts it into a ConnectorDocument with a stable SHA-256-based source_id, then optionally ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

stable_id.purpose: Ensures idempotent syncs — same meeting always produces the same document ID

methods.description: Validates credentials. For Server-to-Server OAuth (account_id present): exchanges credentials for a token via POST /oauth/token, then probes GET /accounts/me. Returns HEALTHY + CONNECTED on success.
Pings GET /accounts/me. Returns HEALTHY, DEGRADED (transient error, circuit not open), or OFFLINE (auth failure or circuit open). Updates circuit breaker state.

methods.signature: async def sync(full: bool = False, since: datetime | None = None, kb_id: str = '') -> SyncResult
methods.description: Paginated full sync of meetings and recordings. Returns documents_found, documents_synced, documents_failed, and a SyncStatus (COMPLETED / PARTIAL / FAILED).

methods.signature: async def list_users(status: str = 'active', page_size: int = 300) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a page of Zoom users in the account.

methods.signature: async def list_meetings(user_id: str = 'me', meeting_type: str = 'scheduled', page_size: int = 300) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a page of Zoom meetings for the given user.

methods.signature: async def list_recordings(user_id: str = 'me', from_date: str | None = None, to_date: str | None = None) -> dict
methods.description: Returns a page of cloud recordings for the given user.
Returns webinars for the given user.
Closes the underlying httpx.AsyncClient and clears the http_client reference.

sections.title: HTTP Client Methods
sections.description: ZoomHTTPClient provides low-level access to the Zoom REST API.

methods.description: POST /oauth/token with account_credentials grant using BasicAuth (client_id:client_secret). Returns and caches the access_token. Propagates it into the config dict.

methods.signature: async def get_users(status: str = 'active', page_size: int = 300, next_page_token: str = '') -> dict
async def get_meetings(user_id: str = 'me', type: str = 'scheduled', page_size: int = 300, next_page_token: str = '') -> dict
async def get_recordings(user_id: str = 'me', from_date: str = '', to_date: str = '', page_size: int = 300, next_page_token: str = '') -> dict
methods.description: GET /users/{user_id}/recordings with date range and pagination.

methods.signature: async def get_webinars(user_id: str = 'me', page_size: int = 300, next_page_token: str = '') -> dict

sections.title: Data Model
sections.description: Every Zoom resource is normalized into a ConnectorDocument before being ingested into the Shielva knowledge base.

fields.name: title
fields.description: Human-readable title. Format: 'Zoom meeting: {topic}' or 'Zoom recording: {topic}'.

fields.name: content
fields.description: Newline-delimited key-value representation of the resource's properties.

fields.name: source_url
fields.description: For meetings: join_url. For recordings: share_url.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Resource-type-specific properties map. Always includes object_type key.

exceptions.name: ZoomError
exceptions.description: Base exception. Carries message, status_code, and code.

exceptions.name: ZoomAuthError
exceptions.description: Raised on 401 Unauthorized or 403 Forbidden. Not retried.

exceptions.name: ZoomRateLimitError
exceptions.description: Raised on 429 Too Many Requests. Carries retry_after (seconds from Retry-After header). Retried after the specified delay.

exceptions.name: ZoomNotFoundError
exceptions.description: Raised on 404 Not Found. Carries resource name and resource path.

exceptions.name: ZoomNetworkError
exceptions.description: Raised on httpx.TimeoutException or httpx.NetworkError. Retried with exponential backoff.

exceptions.name: ZoomServerError
exceptions.description: Raised on 5xx responses from Zoom. Retried.

items.cause: Client ID, Client Secret, or Account ID is incorrect, or the app has been deactivated.
items.solution: Verify credentials in Zoom Marketplace → App Credentials. Re-activate the app if it was deactivated.

items.cause: The Server-to-Server OAuth app is missing one or more required admin scopes.
items.solution: Edit the Zoom App in the Marketplace, add user:read:admin, meeting:read:admin, recording:read:admin, then reactivate.
The connector retries automatically with exponential backoff. For large accounts, schedule syncs during off-peak hours.

items.cause: Transient network errors have tripped the circuit breaker (5 consecutive failures).
items.solution: Resolve the underlying network or credential issue, then trigger a health check to reset the circuit breaker.

items.cause: Free Zoom plan does not support cloud recordings, or recording:read:admin scope is missing.
items.solution: Upgrade to Pro, Business, or Enterprise plan and ensure the recording:read:admin scope is granted.

items.cause: One or more Zoom resources returned unexpected data that caused normalization to fail.
items.solution: Check connector logs for which record IDs failed. Inspect those resources in Zoom for corrupted or null property values.
